title: Computer Architecture
description: Instruction set design, microarchitectural tradeoffs, and performance optimization across the hardware stack.

title: RISC-V Systems
description: Open ISA as a substrate for systems research — bare-metal bring-up, custom extensions, and toolchain construction.

title: Operating System Design
description: Kernel internals, virtual memory, process scheduling, and the mechanisms that bridge hardware to userspace.

title: Processor Microarchitecture
description: Pipeline stages, cache hierarchies, branch predictors, and the micro-level choices that determine real-world throughput.

title: Group-Theoretic Mathematical Modeling
description: Applying algebraic structures — permutation groups, stabilizers, cyclic actions in Sn — to formally model OS primitives such as synchronization, scheduling fairness, and concurrency invariants.
